---
title: "Issue Update"
date: today
format:
  gfm:
    preview-mode: raw
engine: knitr
---

## Update — `2026-03-20`

**Phase:** Fix
**Status:** In Progress

---

## Solution

### Approach

GCTA's `--mlma-loco` internally recalculates allele frequencies and re-applies the MAF threshold,
causing LOCO to test fewer markers than INBRED even when both receive the same PLINK binaries.
Removing `--maf` alone is insufficient because GCTA has a non-zero default threshold (0.01) with
uncertain exact behaviour, and GRM/GWA consistency requires both to operate on the same marker set.

The fix uses `--extract` to pass GCTA a single-column SNP list derived directly from the PLINK
`.bim` file (column 2, SNP IDs set by `--set-missing-var-ids @:#`). This makes PLINK the single
source of truth for marker inclusion: GCTA's internal MAF recalculation still runs algorithmically
but its filtering step has no effect — all markers in the extract list are included regardless.

### Changes Made

**`modules/gcta/make_grm/main.nf` — GRM build:**

```bash
# before
gcta64 --bfile TO_SIMS_... \
        --autosome --maf ${maf} ${GRM_OPTION} \
        ...

# after
awk '{print \$2}' TO_SIMS_....bim > plink_snplist.txt

gcta64 --bfile TO_SIMS_... \
        --autosome --extract plink_snplist.txt ${GRM_OPTION} \
        ...
```

**`modules/gcta/perform_gwa/main.nf` — GWA call (sparse GRM and PCA steps unchanged):**

```bash
# before
gcta64 ${COMMAND} \
    --bfile TO_SIMS_... \
    ...
    --maf ${maf} \
    --thread-num ${GWA_THREADS}

# after
awk '{print \$2}' TO_SIMS_....bim > plink_snplist.txt

gcta64 ${COMMAND} \
    --bfile TO_SIMS_... \
    ...
    --extract plink_snplist.txt \
    --thread-num ${GWA_THREADS}
```

### Correction — Nextflow string interpolation

Initial implementation used `awk '{print $2}'`. Nextflow's Groovy string engine interpolates `$2`
as a variable reference inside the script block heredoc, causing a `token recognition error` at
compilation:

```
ERROR ~ Module compilation error
- file : .../modules/gcta/make_grm/main.nf
- cause: token recognition error at: '2' @ line 31, column 18.
       awk '{print $2}' TO_SIMS_...bim > plink_snplist.txt
                    ^
```

Fixed by escaping: `awk '{print \$2}'`. The `\$` passes a literal `$2` to the shell.

### Rationale

Both GCTA modules already stage the `.bim` in their work directories, so no channel wiring changes
are needed. With `--extract`, LOCO and INBRED will both test the full 7016 PLINK-filtered markers.
Combined with the PR #121 fix (`nrow(mapping_data)` for the BF denominator), the BF thresholds
will also match between modes. Integration test fixtures (`db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv`)
must be re-collected after a full pipeline run.

---

## Validation

### Test Run

**Command:**

```bash
# Wiring check
nextflow run main.nf -profile test,docker -stub-run

# Full run + fixture re-collection
nextflow run main.nf -profile test,docker
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --collect-only
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

**Executor:** local
**Resume from cache:** no (clean run)

### GCTA Log Check

After the pipeline run, verify SNP counts directly from GCTA's `.command.log` files in the
Nextflow work directory.

**LOCO** — `--mlma-loco` logs the total SNPs saved at the end of the run. Before the fix this
line reads `6602 SNPs`; after the fix it must read `7016 SNPs`.

**INBRED** — `--fastGWA-mlm-exact` logs `N SNPs have been processed` at the end. This should
already read `7016 SNPs` both before and after the fix (fastGWA does not apply an internal MAF
re-filter when `--maf` is omitted).

```{bash}
# Set WORK_DIR to wherever Nextflow wrote its work directory.
# Default is relative to this file's location (../../work = project root work/).
# collect_test_data.sh uses tests/.nf-work; override: WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work quarto render ...
WORK_DIR="${WORK_DIR:-../../work}"

echo "=== LOCO: SNPs saved by --mlma-loco ==="
# For each GCTA_PERFORM_GWA LOCO work dir, print its "Saving results" line.
# After the fix every line must contain 7016, not 6602.
find "$WORK_DIR" -name ".command.sh" \
  | xargs grep -l "mlma-loco" 2>/dev/null \
  | while read -r cmd; do
      log="$(dirname "$cmd")/.command.log"
      grep "Saving the results of the mixed linear model association analyses of" \
          "$log" 2>/dev/null
    done | sort -u

echo ""
echo "=== INBRED: SNPs processed by --fastGWA-mlm-exact ==="
# For each GCTA_PERFORM_GWA INBRED work dir, print its "SNPs have been processed" line.
find "$WORK_DIR" -name ".command.sh" \
  | xargs grep -l "fastGWA-mlm-exact" 2>/dev/null \
  | while read -r cmd; do
      log="$(dirname "$cmd")/.command.log"
      grep "SNPs have been processed" "$log" 2>/dev/null
    done | sort -u
```

> **Expected output (after fix):**
> ```
> === LOCO: SNPs saved by --mlma-loco ===
> Saving the results ... of 7016 SNPs to [...]
>
> === INBRED: SNPs processed by --fastGWA-mlm-exact ===
> 7016 SNPs have been processed. Saved 7016 SNPs.
> ```
> Any line showing `6602` in the LOCO block indicates the fix did not take effect for that task.

### Pass / Fail

- [X] Pipeline completed without error
- [X] GCTA LOCO logs show 7016 SNPs included (no MAF-filter reduction)
- [X] GCTA INBRED logs still show 7016 SNPs
- [X] `nrow(mapping_data)` == 7016 for both LOCO and INBRED
- [X] Integration fixtures re-collected and tests pass
- [X] No regression in previously passing steps

---

*Comment generated from `comment.qmd` template · nemascan-sims-nf issue workflow*
